actions-list.actionItem: The House adjourned pursuant to a previous special order. The next meeting is scheduled for 12:00 p.m. on April 20, 2026.
On motion to adjourn Agreed to by voice vote.
Mr. Scott, Austin moved that the House do now adjourn.

Mr. Scott, Austin asked unanimous consent That, when the House adjourns on Friday, April 17, 2026, it adjourn to meet on Monday, April 20, 2026, at noon for Morning-Hour debate and 2:00 p.m. for legislative business. Agreed to without objection.
Motion to reconsider laid on the table Agreed to without objection.
On passage Passed without objection.

Considered by unanimous consent. H.R. 8322 — "To amend the FISA Amendments Act of 2008 to extend the authorities of title VII of the Foreign Intelligence Surveillance Act of 1978 through April 30, 2026, and for other purposes."
Mr. Scott, Austin asked unanimous consent to discharge from committee and consider.
Motion to reconsider laid on the table Agreed to without objection.

actions-list.header: On agreeing to the resolution Failed by recorded vote: 197
On agreeing to the Scott, Austin amendment; Failed by the Yeas and Nays: 200
On ordering the previous question on the amendment and the resolution Agreed to by the Yeas and Nays: 211

actions-list.actionItem: An amendment, offered by Mr. Scott, Austin,  to strike the first section after the resolving clause and insert a new paragraph providing for the consideration of H.R. 8035.
The House proceeded with one hour of debate on H. Res. 1175.

Considered as privileged matter. H. Res. 1175 — "Providing for consideration of the bill (H.R. 8035) to amend the FISA Amendments Act of 2008 to extend the authorities of title VII of the Foreign Intelligence Surveillance Act of 1978 through October 20, 2027, and for other purposes."
The House convened, returning from a recess continuing the legislative day of April 16.
The Speaker announced that the House do now recess. The next meeting is subject to the call of the Chair.

actions-list.header: The Speaker laid before the House a message from the President transmitting a notification stating that the national emergency declared in Proclamation 10371 of April 21, 2022, with respect to the Russian Federation and the emergency authority relating to the regulation of the anchorage and movement of Russian-affiliated vessels to United States ports, is to continue in effect beyond April 21, 2026
actions-list.actionItem: referred to the Committee on Foreign Affairs and ordered to be printed (H. Doc. 119-149).
Motion to reconsider laid on the table Agreed to without objection.

actions-list.header: On agreeing to the resolution Agreed to by the Yeas and Nays: 219

actions-list.actionItem: Considered as unfinished business. H. Res. 1156 — "Expressing support for tax policies that support working families."
Motion to reconsider laid on the table Agreed to without objection.

actions-list.header: On passage Passed by the Yeas and Nays: 224

actions-list.actionItem: The previous question was ordered pursuant to the rule.
The House resumed debate on H.R. 1689.
The House received a message from the Senate. The Senate passed H.R. 2066 and H.J. Res. 140.
The House proceeded with one hour of debate on H.R. 1689.
Considered under the provisions of rule H. Res. 965. H.R. 1689 — "To require the Secretary of Homeland Security to designate Haiti for temporary protected status."
Motion to reconsider laid on the table Agreed to without objection.

actions-list.header: On agreeing to the resolution Agreed to by the Yeas and Nays: 220

actions-list.actionItem: Considered as unfinished business. H. Res. 965 — "Providing for consideration of the bill (H.R. 1689) to require the Secretary of Homeland Security to designate Haiti for temporary protected status."
Motion to reconsider laid on the table Agreed to without objection.

actions-list.header: On passage Passed by the Yeas and Nays: 222
On motion to recommit Failed by the Yeas and Nays: 213

actions-list.actionItem: Considered as unfinished business. H.R. 6398 — "To amend the Clean Air Act relating to review by the Environmental Protection Agency of proposed legislation."
Motion to reconsider laid on the table Agreed to without objection.

actions-list.header: On passage Passed by the Yeas and Nays: 220
On motion to recommit Failed by the Yeas and Nays: 213

actions-list.actionItem: Considered as unfinished business. H.R. 6409 — "To amend the Clean Air Act to clarify standards for emissions emanating from outside of the United States, and for other purposes."
Motion to reconsider laid on the table Agreed to without objection.

actions-list.header: On agreeing to the resolution Failed by the Yeas and Nays: 213
actions-list.actionItem: 214, 1 Present (Roll no. 114).
The previous question was ordered pursuant to a previous  order of the House.
Pursuant to a previous order, the House proceeded with one hour of debate on H. Con. Res. 40.

Considered pursuant to a previous order. H. Con. Res. 40 — "Directing the President, pursuant to section 5(c) of the War Powers Resolution, to remove United States Armed Forces from hostilities with Iran."
Consideration initiated pursuant to a previous order.
The House proceeded with one minute speeches, which by direction of the Chair would be limited to 15 per side of the aisle.
The Chair designated Ms. Ocasio-Cortez to lead the Members in reciting the Pledge of Allegiance to the Flag.
The Speaker announced approval of the Journal.  Pursuant to clause 1, rule I, the Journal stands approved.
Today's prayer was offered by the House Chaplain, Chaplain Margaret Grun Kibben.
The House convened, starting a new legislative day.
